hypothesis: the current production clipboard failure comes from the remote controller swallowing shortcut keys and lacking explicit copy/cut/select-all actions
action: initialized the clipboard-bridge tracker, inspected the frontend/backend remote action paths, and captured baseline production health, remote status, and session inventory artifacts
expected_signal: the code inspection should show shortcut interception before paste, no copy/cut/select-all actions, and baseline production artifacts saved before any edits
result: confirmed: the frontend keydown handler prevents default for modified keys before paste, the backend only supports text_input/paste_text/key_down/key_up/navigate, and the baseline production artifacts were saved successfully
decision: implement a proper clipboard bridge with explicit remote actions and action-result payloads, then prove the live facebook shortcut flow in production

hypothesis: the clipboard bridge implementation is locally sound if the focused clipboard tests and the full backend/frontend gate stay green from the current worktree
action: implemented the frontend clipboard shortcut bridge, backend clipboard actions, and focused tests; then ran backend pytest plus frontend test/lint/build while teeing outputs into docs/execution/remote-browser-clipboard-bridge/artifacts/local/
expected_signal: frontend tests should prove meta/ctrl clipboard shortcuts and error paths, backend tests should prove selection semantics, and the full local gate should pass without introducing new lint errors
result: confirmed: backend finished at 370 passed, frontend tests finished at 21 passed, build passed, and lint reported only the existing 7 App.tsx warnings while the new clipboard-focused tests passed in both layers
decision: promote the current worktree to deployment candidacy and move to production verification through the live frontend and backend
playbook_note: for remote-controller input changes, require focused shortcut tests first and then the full backend/frontend gate; the shortcut tests catch user-gesture and modifier-state bugs before the broader suite does

hypothesis: the clipboard bridge is production-safe only if the live deployed head can prove facebook composer copy/paste/cut/select-all and reddit typing after railway finishes the final backend deploy
action: verified that origin/main advanced to d7834d8 while retaining the clipboard bridge, waited for railway and vercel success on that exact head, reran the facebook proof on the live production controller after deploy completion, captured remote logs and screenshots, verified reddit typing on the same deployed build, confirmed remote status returned to zero after close, and deleted the temporary verifier account
expected_signal: facebook should copy probe text back to the operator clipboard, paste a unique sentinel into the live composer via cmd+v, cut only after the clipboard already contains the sentinel, reddit should still accept typed input, and sessions/remote/status should report zero active leases after close
result: confirmed on deployed commit d7834d84e9e52c30efe47c9c6d497862e49b04e6: facebook composer focus was proven through textarea focus snapshots and typed input, copy returned proof-d783 to the operator clipboard, paste inserted sentinel-d783-20260312 into the composer, cut deleted only after the clipboard already contained the sentinel, reddit typed reddit-regression-0312 successfully, and the remote status endpoint returned count 0 after both controllers closed
decision: mark the clipboard bridge complete and ship the proof artifacts with the tracker update
playbook_note: if production main moves during verification, rerun the user-facing proof on the final deployed commit after railway marks that head successful; do not rely on evidence captured across a deployment cutover
